basic_details.job_title: MHRB Recruitment 2026 - Apply Offline for Psychiatrist, Medical Practitioner and More Posts
basic_details.post_name: Member, Dhule Mental Health Review Board
basic_details.short_information: Mental Health Review Board (MHRB) Member, Dhule Mental Health Review Board Notification 2026 has been released on the official website. A total of 4 vacancies have been announced through the MHRB Recruitment 2026 for Psychiatrist, Medical Practitioner, and Member (Persons with Mental Illness/Care-givers/NGOs) posts.

age_limit.details: For All Posts: Maximum age limit is 70 years.

qualification.education_qualification: Post-Graduate Degree or Diploma in Psychiatry with 15 years experience, Recognized medical qualification with 15 years experience, Care-giver or NGO representative with 10 years experience in mental health
qualification.details: Psychiatrist: Medical Practitioner having Post-Graduate Degree or Diploma in Psychiatry by a university recognized by the UGC or by National Board of Examinations (NBE) or by Medical Council of India with at least 15 years' experience in field. Medical Practitioner: Medical Practitioner means a person who possesses the recognized medical qualification as defined in clause (h) of section 2 of the Indian Medical Council Act, 1956, and whose name has been entered in the State Medical Register, or as defined in clause (h) of sub-section (1) of section 2 of the Indian Medicine Central Council Act, 1970, and whose name has been entered in a State Register of Indian Medicine, or as defined in clause (g) of sub-section (1) of section 2 of the Homoeopathy Central Council Act, 1973, and whose name has been entered in a State Register of Homoeopathy, with at least 15 years' experience in field. Member (Persons with mental illness or care-givers or NGOs): Person who resides with a person with mental illness and is responsible for providing a care to that person and includes a relative or any other person/Organization representing Care Givers OR Representatives of registered NGOs with 10 years' experience in the field of Mental Health.

vacancy_details.Post Name: Member (Persons with mental illness or care-givers or NGOs)

post_wise_breakdown.post_name: Member (Persons with mental illness or care-givers or NGOs)

how_to_apply: Candidates who wish to apply for MHRB Various Posts Recruitment 2026 through offline mode should follow the steps below.
Read the official notification carefully before applying.
Download the application form from the official website: .
Fill the application form carefully with correct information.
Attach self-attested copies of required documents such as photograph, signature, ID proof, and certificates.
Check all details carefully before submission.
Send the completed application form to the address mentioned in the notification through Speed Post / Registered Post or as instructed.
Ensure the application reaches before the last date: 06-07-2026.
Keep a photocopy of the submitted application form for future reference.

content_sections.heading: MHRB Vacancy 2026 Notification PDF
content_sections.text: Candidates should download and read the MHRB Jobs 2026 notification PDF carefully to check vacancy details, eligibility, age limit, and application instructions. The notification also includes important dates and selection process details. The direct download link is available in the Important Links section below. Download MHRB Job 2026 Notification PDF

rows.post_name: Member (Persons with mental illness or care-givers or NGOs)

content_sections.heading: Eligibility Criteria
content_sections.text: Psychiatrist: Medical Practitioner having Post-Graduate Degree or Diploma in Psychiatry by a university recognized by the UGC or by National Board of Examinations (NBE) or by Medical Council of India with at least 15 years' experience in field. Medical Practitioner: Medical Practitioner means a person who possesses the recognized medical qualification as defined in clause (h) of section 2 of the Indian Medical Council Act, 1956, and whose name has been entered in the State Medical Register, or as defined in clause (h) of sub-section (1) of section 2 of the Indian Medicine Central Council Act, 1970, and whose name has been entered in a State Register of Indian Medicine, or as defined in clause (g) of sub-section (1) of section 2 of the Homoeopathy Central Council Act, 1973, and whose name has been entered in a State Register of Homoeopathy, with at least 15 years' experience in field. Member (Persons with mental illness or care-givers or NGOs): Person who resides with a person with mental illness and is responsible for providing a care to that person and includes a relative or any other person/Organization representing Care Givers OR Representatives of registered NGOs with 10 years' experience in the field of Mental Health.

content_sections.heading: Age Limit
content_sections.text: For All Posts: Maximum age limit is 70 years.

content_sections.heading: How to Apply
content_sections.text: Candidates who wish to apply for MHRB Various Posts Recruitment 2026 through offline mode should follow the steps below. Read the official notification carefully before applying. Download the application form from the official website: . Fill the application form carefully with correct information. Attach self-attested copies of required documents such as photograph, signature, ID proof, and certificates. Check all details carefully before submission. Send the completed application form to the address mentioned in the notification through Speed Post / Registered Post or as instructed. Ensure the application reaches before the last date: 06-07-2026. Keep a photocopy of the submitted application form for future reference.

faq.question: Q1. What is the total number of vacancies in MHRB Recruitment 2026?
faq.answer: There are a total of 4 vacancies for the Member, Dhule Mental Health Review Board posts in the MHRB Recruitment 2026, offering opportunities for eligible candidates.

faq.question: Q2. What is the last date to apply for MHRB Member posts?
faq.answer: The last date for submitting the offline application for MHRB Member posts is 06 July 2026. Candidates are advised to complete and send their applications well in advance to avoid any last-minute issues.

faq.question: Q3. What is the application mode for MHRB Recruitment 2026?
faq.answer: The application mode for MHRB Recruitment 2026 is offline. Interested candidates need to download the application form from the official website, fill it out, and submit it via post.

faq.question: Q4. What are the age limit criteria for MHRB Member posts?
faq.answer: Candidates applying for the MHRB Member posts must not exceed 70 years of age. There is no specific minimum age mentioned in the official notification for these positions.

faq.question: Q5. What qualifications are required for the Psychiatrist post?
faq.answer: For the Psychiatrist post, a Post-Graduate Degree or Diploma in Psychiatry from a recognized university or board, along with at least 15 years of experience in the field, is required.

faq.question: Q6. Is there any application fee for MHRB Recruitment 2026?
faq.answer: No, there is no application fee mentioned for any category, including General/OBC and SC/ST, for the MHRB Recruitment 2026. Candidates can apply without paying any fee.

faq.question: Q7. What is the selection process for MHRB Member positions?
faq.answer: The selection process for MHRB Member positions primarily involves an initial application review to assess eligibility, followed by a nomination process based on the evaluated applications.

faq.question: Q8. What is the job type for the MHRB Member posts?
faq.answer: The job type for the MHRB Member, Dhule Mental Health Review Board posts is contractual. This offers a specific tenure for the selected candidates as per the terms of the contract.

faq.question: Q9. Where can I find the official notification for MHRB Recruitment?
faq.answer: The official notification PDF for MHRB Recruitment 2026 can be downloaded directly from the Important Links section provided in this article. It contains all essential details for applicants.

faq.question: Q10. What experience is needed for the Medical Practitioner post?
faq.answer: For the Medical Practitioner post, candidates need a recognized medical qualification as defined in relevant Indian Medical Council Acts, with their name entered in a State Medical Register, and at least 15 years of experience in the field.
